exercises.title: Verify Clifford Group
exercises.description: Verify that H, S, and CNOT generate Clifford operations by showing they map Paulis to Paulis.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

H = np.array([[1,1],[1,-1]])/np.sqrt(2)
S = np.array([[1,0],[0,1j]])
X = np.array([[0,1],[1,0]])
Z = np.array([[1,0],[0,-1]])

def conjugate(G, P):
    '''Compute G·P·G†'''
    return G @ P @ G.conj().T

# Verify H maps X↔Z
print('HXH† =', np.round(conjugate(H, X).real, 2))
print('HZH† =', np.round(conjugate(H, Z).real, 2))
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

H = np.array([[1,1],[1,-1]])/np.sqrt(2)
S = np.array([[1,0],[0,1j]])
X = np.array([[0,1],[1,0]])
Y = np.array([[0,-1j],[1j,0]])
Z = np.array([[1,0],[0,-1]])

def conjugate(G, P):
    return G @ P @ G.conj().T

print('Clifford conjugations:')
print('HXH† = Z:', np.allclose(conjugate(H, X), Z))
print('HZH† = X:', np.allclose(conjugate(H, Z), X))
print('SXS† = Y:', np.allclose(conjugate(S, X), Y))
print('SZS† = Z:', np.allclose(conjugate(S, Z), Z))
hints: Clifford gates permute Paulis under conjugation

exercises.title: T Gate Non-Clifford
exercises.description: Show T is not Clifford by demonstrating TXT† is not a Pauli.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

T = np.array([[1,0],[0,np.exp(1j*np.pi/4)]])
X = np.array([[0,1],[1,0]])
Y = np.array([[0,-1j],[1j,0]])
Z = np.array([[1,0],[0,-1]])

# Compute TXT† and check if it's a Pauli
result = T @ X @ T.conj().T
print('TXT† =')
print(np.round(result, 3))
# Is it X, Y, Z, or ±I?
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

T = np.array([[1,0],[0,np.exp(1j*np.pi/4)]])
X = np.array([[0,1],[1,0]])
Y = np.array([[0,-1j],[1j,0]])
Z = np.array([[1,0],[0,-1]])
I = np.eye(2)

result = T @ X @ T.conj().T
print('TXT† =')
print(np.round(result, 3))

print('\nIs Pauli?')
for name, P in [('I', I), ('X', X), ('Y', Y), ('Z', Z), ('-I', -I), ('-X', -X), ('-Y', -Y), ('-Z', -Z)]:
    if np.allclose(result, P):
        print(f'  = {name}')
        break
else:
    print('  NOT a Pauli! T is non-Clifford.')
hints: TXT† will have complex off-diagonal entries
